title: Podman inside a Docker Container cannot pull from Local Registry to different --root even with fuse-overlayfs storage driver
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am running Podman 3.3.1 inside an Ubuntu 20.04 Docker Container and I cannot pull from local registry to a different specified --root directory, even with fuse-overlayfs  storage driver. Podman complains with this error:

`Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver`

If I do **not** specify the **--root** , podman successfully pulls the image from the local registry.
This worked with my previous version of podman ( 3.2.x ) I believe - on the same machine.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman 3.3.1 inside an Ubuntu 20.04 Docker Container.

2.) Configure the storage.conf files to use the fuse-overlayfs driver:

```
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ uname -r
5.10.47-linuxkit

username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ /usr/local/bin/fuse-overlayfs --version
fuse-overlayfs: version 1.8-dev
FUSE library version 3.9.0
using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
fusermount3 version: 3.9.0

username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ cat storage.conf 
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
mountopt = "nodev"
mount_program = "/usr/local/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
[storage.options.thinpool]

username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ cat /etc/containers/storage.conf 
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/container-storage/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/container-storage/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
mountopt = "nodev"
mount_program = "/usr/local/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
[storage.options.thinpool]
```

3. Setup a local registry and run it with podman.

```
sudo /usr/bin/podman run --privileged -d \
    --name registry \
    --net=registry \
    -p 5000:5000 \
    -v /var/lib/registry:/var/lib/registry \
    --restart=always \
    --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs \
    registry:2
```

4. Create a new directory to pull the images into from the local registry ( --root ):

```
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ sudo rm -rf /home/username/rootless-container-storage/
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ mkdir -p /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage
```

5. Ensure the registry is up and reachable:

```
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS=$(sudo /usr/bin/podman inspect registry | grep IPAddress | grep 10 | cut -d ':' -f 2 | sed 's/,/ /g' | xargs)
 address is ${REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS} ..."
    /usr/bin/curl -v http://$REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/v2/_catalousername@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$     bbwarn "Registry address is ${REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS} ..."
bash: bbwarn: command not found
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$     /usr/bin/curl -v http://$REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/v2/_catalog
*   Trying 10.89.0.2:5000...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to 10.89.0.2 (10.89.0.2) port 5000 (#0)
> GET /v2/_catalog HTTP/1.1
> Host: 10.89.0.2:5000
> User-Agent: curl/7.68.0
> Accept: */*
> 
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
< Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
< X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
< Date: Thu, 23 Sep 2021 13:58:13 GMT
< Content-Length: 102
< 
{"repositories":["aws-iot-greengrass","username-base"]}
* Connection #0 to host 10.89.0.2 left intact
```

6.) Attempt to pull from the registry with the **--root** flag and observe the error:

```
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ sudo -u username /usr/bin/podman --root=/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage pull $REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/username-base:1.4
Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver

username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ sudo /usr/bin/podman --root=/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage pull $REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/username-base:1.4
Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver

username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ /usr/bin/podman --root=/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage pull $REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/username-base:1.4
Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver

username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ sudo -u username /usr/bin/podman --log-level=debug --root=/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage pull $REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/username-base:1.4
INFO[0000] /usr/bin/podman filtering at log level debug 
DEBU[0000] Called pull.PersistentPreRunE(/usr/bin/podman --log-level=debug --root=/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage pull 10.89.0.2:5000/username-base:1.4) 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/libexec/podman/conmon"   
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /tmp/run-1000/libpod/tmp       
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] Not configuring container store              
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend file              
DEBU[0000] configured OCI runtime runc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runc: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/crun"            
INFO[0000] Found CNI network podman (type=bridge) at /home/username/.config/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist 
DEBU[0000] Default CNI network name podman is unchangeable 
INFO[0000] Setting parallel job count to 25             
INFO[0000] /usr/bin/podman filtering at log level debug 
DEBU[0000] Called pull.PersistentPreRunE(/usr/bin/podman --log-level=debug --root=/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage pull 10.89.0.2:5000/username-base:1.4) 
DEBU[0000] cached value indicated that overlay is not supported 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf" 
DEBU[0000] cached value indicated that overlay is not supported 
DEBU[0000] cached value indicated that overlay is not supported 
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/libexec/podman/conmon"   
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Overriding tmp dir "/tmp/podman-run-1000/libpod/tmp" with "/tmp/run-1000/libpod/tmp" from database 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /tmp/run-1000/libpod/tmp       
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] cached value indicated that overlay is not supported 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay" 
Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```

7.) Observe that if you do not specify **--root**, the pull succeeds:

```
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ sudo -u username /usr/bin/podman pull $REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/username-base:1.4Trying to pull 10.89.0.2:5000/username-base:1.4...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 77012d4d0d98 done  
Copying blob 5d624f74833d done  
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:77012d4d0d9832a14548d6f7ed67badce8d824c08905c35fed7b2fbab20d4ce4": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/shadow): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /etc/shadow: invalid argument
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ 
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ sudo /usr/bin/podman pull $REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/username-base:1.4
Trying to pull 10.89.0.2:5000/username-base:1.4...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5d624f74833d skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 77012d4d0d98 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 32adc9d03d done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
32adc9d03d2c21fe6c8182aad4c6816e2fbfa98d0f69400f07357cec7c83abe1
```

**Describe the results you received:**

The error:
`Error: 'overlay' is not supported over overlayfs, a mount_program is required: backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver`

**Describe the results you expected:**

The ability to change the **--root** for podman pull from a local registry.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
username@aac514184bb1:~/.config/containers$ podman --version
podman version 3.3.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

N/A

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Docker on Mac.

comments.body: Can you volume mount in the directory where the --root is pointing at?  That should fix the problem.  If the storage podman is attempting to use in the container is mounted on an overlayfs then it will not be able to use it.

@rhatdan - I volume mounted the directory and now I get this error:

```
username@7dd5cccd5efe:/workspaces$ sudo -u username /usr/bin/podman --root=/home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage pull $REGISTRY_IP_ADDRESS:5000/username-base:1.4
Error: error recording overlay not being supported (kernel does not support overlay fs: unable to create kernel-style whiteout: operation not permitted): open /home/username/rootless-container-storage/containers/storage/overlay/overlay-false: no such file or directory
```

What is interesting to me, is that if I do not specify '**--root**', the the pull works and **podman --info** shows that podman is configured with fuse-overlayfs.

Could you try this with quay.io/podman/stable image or an image built on top of it.

Have you read https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have had no feedback for one month, I am going to close this issue.
